title: American Stovetop Mac and Cheese

subs.name: any short pasta shape — penne, fusilli or farfalle are all fine
any neutral cooking oil, though butter gives the roux its characteristic flavour
evaporated milk for a richer, creamier sauce
Amul processed cheese cubes (widely available in India), or a mix of mozzarella and cream cheese

ingredients.name: mustard seeds, dry-toasted and ground (optional, for depth)

steps.text: Bring a large pot of well-salted water to a rolling boil. Cook the pasta according to packet instructions until al dente — it should have a slight bite since it will cook further in the sauce.
Drain the pasta, reserving half a cup of pasta water. Set aside. Do not rinse the pasta.
Melt the butter in the same pot on medium flame. Add the maida and stir constantly for 1–2 minutes until the raw flour smell disappears and the roux turns a pale straw colour.
Pour in the milk in a slow stream, whisking to prevent lumps. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook, stirring, until the sauce coats the back of a spoon, about 3–4 minutes.
Remove from the flame and stir in the grated cheese until completely melted. Add the black pepper, ground mustard (if using) and salt. The sauce should be thick, glossy and smooth.

Return the drained pasta to the pot and fold through the cheese sauce. If it feels too thick, add a splash of the reserved pasta water. Heat on low for 1 minute until everything is piping hot, then serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cheese → Amul processed cheese cubes (widely available in India), or a mix of mozzarella and cream cheese (any good-melting cheese works); milk → evaporated milk for a richer, creamier sauce; pasta → any short pasta shape — penne, fusilli or farfalle are all fine; butter → any neutral cooking oil, though butter gives the roux its characteristic flavour
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Use a kadai instead of a saucepan — the wide base makes whisking the roux easier. Amul cheese cubes (full box, chopped fine) melt well and give a mild, pleasantly salty flavour. If you want a sharper bite, mix in a tbsp of thick curd (dahi) at the end.
